title: Prefer real rain sensors over broad weather warnings
problem: Outdoor automations can behave poorly when rain detection relies only on generic weather states or broad heat and storm warnings.
solution: Use a local rain sensor as the primary block condition, keep severe rain warnings as secondary protection, and avoid restarting a running mower workflow from the beginning on each trigger.
evidence: Validated from a Home Assistant mower automation review where the desired behavior was to mow in heat but stop for actual rain.
risk: Sensor entities and weather warning semantics vary by installation and must be checked before applying this pattern.
